content:
- Read progress log and current task summaries.
- Found one active bug task, but it appears blocked by four backup-removal tasks; now verifying those blockers in current_tasks_done.
- Also inspecting the main unblocked candidate story entry tasks so task choice follows precedence plus actual codebase direction instead of list order.

- Confirmed the backup-removal blockers are recorded in current_tasks_done; now checking their exact passes tags.
- If they are true, the active bug task becomes unblocked and must be selected immediately under the mandatory bug-first rule.
- Preparing to write only that task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and then stop.

- Verified all four backup-removal blockers are marked <passes>true</passes> in current_tasks_done.
- That unblocks the active bug task about static rewind/basebackup source selection.
- Selecting the bug now because bug-first is mandatory and it outranks all non-bug tasks.

- Read current task pointer and opened bug task for rewind/basebackup source selection.
- Determined this is the first planning pass because the task file has no TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker yet.
- Started code inspection focused on runtime/node, ha/process_dispatch, config surface, and tests for static rewind/basebackup sources.

- Confirmed `process_defaults_from_config` in `src/runtime/node.rs` hardcodes both basebackup and rewind source host/port from `postgres.rewind_source_*`.
- Confirmed `src/ha/process_dispatch.rs` uses those fixed defaults for `HaAction::StartBaseBackup` and `HaAction::StartRewind`.
- Inspecting HA state and DCS cache/member metadata to identify the right action-time source derivation point and whether startup clone path needs the same treatment.

- DCS state inspection shows `MemberRecord` only carries role/health/LSN/timeline metadata; no remote endpoint fields are present.
- Need to identify whether leader source derivation can reuse existing config or requires extending DCS member metadata with host/port.
- Searching schema and call sites for any existing per-member connection target surface before writing the plan.

- Confirmed there is no current cluster-wide source-of-truth for a leader endpoint besides each node's local `listen_host`/`listen_port` values.
- Investigating DCS worker publish paths and existing tests to confirm whether the clean fix is extending `MemberRecord` and then deriving source conninfo from current leader member metadata at dispatch/startup time.

- DCS worker writes `MemberRecord` through `write_local_member`, so any endpoint fields added there will propagate cluster-wide and into HA/runtime startup decisions.
- Now surveying process dispatch tests plus all config/docs references to `rewind_source_host/port` so the task plan can explicitly cover schema/parser/doc cleanup and leadership-change tests.

- Final planning pass: inspecting process job spec types and operator docs to decide the clean end-state.
- Expectation now is to keep role/auth/identity defaults, remove static source host/port config, and derive remote endpoint host/port from the leader member record for both startup clone and HA dispatch actions.

- Wrote the first-pass execution plan into the current bug task file.
- Plan explicitly calls for extending DCS `MemberRecord` with member postgres endpoint data, removing static `rewind_source_host/port` config, deriving basebackup/rewind sources from current leader facts, and updating tests/docs.
- Added the required `TO BE VERIFIED` marker and stopped without executing, per task instructions.

- Re-entered the rewind/basebackup-source bug task and found the task file still at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Starting the skeptical review pass now: reading the task file plus the referenced runtime, HA, DCS, config, and docs code to validate the plan and change it materially before any execution phase begins.
- Need to confirm whether the planned shared derivation helper and DCS member endpoint schema are really the cleanest boundary, and whether any execution ordering inside the plan should change.

- Skeptical review found a substantive flaw in the written plan: it over-shared leader selection between startup and HA dispatch.
- Startup really does have its own selection policy (`leader` key first, init-lock healthy-primary fallback), but HA recovery decisions already carry an exact `leader_member_id` in `RecoveryStrategy`; dispatch should preserve and use that chosen member, not re-resolve a fresh leader from cache.
- Updating the task file plan now so the shared code only assembles source conninfo from a member endpoint plus role-specific defaults, while startup selection and HA dispatch target selection stay distinct.

- Implemented the first core pass: new `ha::source_conn` helper module, `MemberRecord` now carries postgres endpoint fields, startup clone source is derived from the selected member record, and HA recovery actions now carry `leader_member_id`.
- First `cargo check` surfaced a few immediate compile issues only: `thiserror` strings on `MemberId` fields need string fields instead, `ProcessDispatchError::SourceSelection` must be mapped in `ha/apply.rs`, and `resolve_source_member` needs to return an owned member rather than a reference into a short-lived snapshot.
- Fixing those compile errors next, then I will sweep the much larger config-fixture fallout from removing `rewind_source_host/port`.

- Read prior progress log and scanned docs/src for stale rewind/basebackup source guidance.
- Found only docs/src/operator/configuration.md and docs/src/operator/troubleshooting.md referencing rewind_source_host/port.
- Reading surrounding doc sections now, then will patch only docs/src/** to describe DCS-derived source host/port with credentials still coming from config.

- Patched docs/src/operator/configuration.md to remove static rewind_source_host/port from the example and field descriptions.
- Added operator guidance that basebackup/rewind source host/port come from the current leader/member PostgreSQL endpoint in DCS, while role-specific credentials still come from config.
- Patched troubleshooting guidance to check DCS leader/member endpoint records and connectivity instead of stale rewind_source_host/port config.

- Verified with an exact rg search that docs/src no longer contains rewind_source_host or rewind_source_port.
- Reviewed the final diff: only docs/src/operator/configuration.md and docs/src/operator/troubleshooting.md changed.
- Ready to report docs-only completion with a short summary of the DCS-derived endpoint behavior and config-sourced credentials.

- Resumed the NOW EXECUTE phase of the rewind/basebackup-source bug task.
- Confirmed the previous exact long-test session is no longer running; the remaining blocker is still the multi-node unassisted failover SQL consistency test.
- Next step is to rerun that exact test with the Makefile target-dir settings, inspect the timeline/log evidence if it still fails, and then push through make test-long and make lint.

- Re-ran the exact long failover scenario with the Makefile target-dir settings after tightening `ha::decide` recovery target selection.
- The previously failing `e2e_multi_node_unassisted_failover_sql_consistency` test now passes in about 54s.
- Next step is the mandatory full gate sequence: make check, make test, make test-long, then make lint, before updating task completion state.

- Diagnosed the shared HA regression behind the current ultra-long failures by reading the failing stress/failsafe timelines plus src/ha/{decision,decide,apply,process_dispatch,source_conn}.rs.
- Strongest evidence is the no-quorum failure timeline showing node-1 in fail_safe while SQL reports FATAL missing global/pg_filenode.map; that points to an unintended recovery wipe/basebackup path, not just API flakiness.
- Most likely root cause is a mismatch between leader eligibility in DecisionFacts (`active_leader_member_id` only checks member.sql Healthy) and source validation in process dispatch (`source_conn` requires MemberRole::Primary + Healthy), combined with apply.rs wiping the data dir before validating/dispatching StartBaseBackup.
- Need to report exact code locations: decision.rs lines around active_leader_member_id/is_available_primary_leader, decide.rs lines using recovery_leader_member_id, apply.rs lines 231-249 WipeDataDir before StartBaseBackup, process_dispatch.rs lines 122-139 + source_conn.rs lines 46-69.

- Resumed in execute phase for rewind/basebackup-source task after restoring context.
- Re-read Ralph progress log and confirmed current task pointer still targets the same bug task file.
- Next step is to rerun the mandatory full gate sequence from the current tree: make check, make test, make test-long, then make lint.

- `make check` passed cleanly on the current tree during NOW EXECUTE.
- Proceeding to the next mandatory gate: `make test`.
- If unit/integration regressions appear, fix them before attempting long tests or lint.

- `make test` failed on one HA worker integration test: `integration_primary_split_brain_enters_fencing_and_process_feedback_advances`.
- Failure shows the worker stayed in `Fencing` after process feedback where the test expected `WaitingDcsTrusted`.
- Reading the test and the current fencing decision path now to determine whether the test expectation or implementation needs to change.

- Patched the failing split-brain worker integration test.
- The test now queues two process successes and advances process/HA twice because split-brain dispatches demote and fencing as separate jobs, and the new phase-aware outcome tracking only treats the fencing outcome as fencing completion.
- Verifying the exact test before rerunning the full `make test` gate.

- The split-brain worker integration test now passes.
- The actual invariant was that the first successful process job keeps HA in `Fencing`, and only the subsequent successful fencing job advances to `WaitingDcsTrusted`.
- Rerunning the full `make test` gate now.

- `make test` now passes completely after updating the split-brain worker integration test to account for separate demote and fencing jobs.
- Starting the mandatory `make test-long` gate next.
- After that, only `make lint` and the task-finalization flow should remain if no new regressions appear.

- `make test-long` still has one failing ultra-long scenario: `e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity`.
- Failure is substantive: 48 commits landed after the fail-safe fencing cutoff where only 10 are tolerated.
- Investigating the fencing/fail-safe timing path now via the test source, emitted timeline evidence, and HA decision/apply code.

- `make test-long` still has one failing ultra-long case: `e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity`.
- Failure is not random noise: the summary reports 48 commits after the fencing cutoff with only 10 allowed.
- Investigating the no-quorum fencing path now by reading the test, the fail-safe/fencing code, and the generated timeline/summary evidence.

- Patched fail-safe lowering so the initial no-quorum transition from `Primary` now dispatches a real fencing job in addition to releasing the leader lease.
- Updated HA lower/worker tests to cover the new fail-safe fencing behavior.
- Targeted unit/integration checks pass; running the exact failing ultra-long no-quorum fencing scenario now.

- Tightened the no-quorum failure diagnosis.
- `EnterFailSafe { release_leader_lease: true }` already lowers to `FenceNode`, so the long-test failure is not a pure no-op fail-safe path.
- Most likely root cause is one-shot fail-safe fencing: if the first fencing dispatch lands while the process worker is still busy with an active promote/start job, the request is rejected, HA still transitions to `FailSafe`, and later no-quorum ticks stop asking for fencing because `release_leader_lease` becomes false once the current phase is no longer `Primary`.

- Patched the no-quorum decision path to keep emitting fail-safe fencing while local Postgres is still primary, instead of stopping after the first phase transition into `FailSafe`.
- Added a new decision unit test that explicitly covers retrying fail-safe fencing from the `FailSafe` phase when local SQL is still primary.
- Verifying unit tests first, then the exact failing long scenario.

- The no-quorum long test was still deleting the leader lease because the top-level fail-safe branch in `src/ha/decide.rs` still computed `release_leader_lease` from current leader/primary facts.
- Patched that branch to force `release_leader_lease: false`; fail-safe still lowers to fencing, but it no longer deletes the leader key.
- Next step is a clean sequential verification run in a fresh target dir to confirm the exact no-quorum fencing scenario finally stays dark after cutoff.

- After the no-quorum retry patch, the remaining `make test` failures were expectation drift in decide/worker tests around fail-safe lease release.
- Updated those tests so primary/no-quorum paths now expect lease release plus fencing, while non-primary fail-safe paths remain lease-neutral.
- Fresh `make test` now passes completely again; rerunning `make test-long` next.

- The full `make test-long` rerun showed the next real failure mode: all nodes were being fenced in fail-safe, leaving no node queryable for post-window integrity verification.
- That came from `lower.rs` always mapping `EnterFailSafe` to `FenceNode`; I restored the conditional mapping so only release-required fail-safe decisions emit fencing and non-primary fail-safe stays coordination-only.
- Verifying the exact no-quorum fencing scenario again before rerunning the full long gate.

- Investigating the failing long test the user called out without editing files.
- Read progress and confirmed current Ralph task pointer; now reading the long test, HA fail-safe/fencing code, and any saved long-test evidence artifacts.
- Goal is a concise root-cause diagnosis with exact file/line locations to change.

- Investigation complete for the failing long no-quorum fencing test.
- Strongest direct culprit in current tree: src/ha/decide.rs no-quorum branch emits EnterFailSafe { release_leader_lease: false } even when local node is/was primary, and src/ha/lower.rs maps that to SignalFailSafe instead of FenceNode.
- Secondary reliability gap: HA advances phase even when a safety action later faults, while process busy rejections are not reflected in ProcessState, so missed fencing is not robustly retried.

- Resumed execution on the rewind/basebackup-source task in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Re-read Ralph progress plus the task file. The remaining blocker is still the no-quorum fail-safe long test; latest known exact failure was commits_after_cutoff=14 allowed=10.
- The latest code patch makes FailSafe sticky while fencing is running or while local postgres is still primary. Next step is to rerun the focused HA decide tests and then the exact failing long test before rerunning the full mandatory gates.

- Found the current tree still had the old no-quorum primary behavior in src/ha/decide.rs: EnterFailSafe { release_leader_lease: false } only signaled fail-safe and did not lower to fencing.
- Patched decide.rs so primary/leader no-quorum enters FailSafe with release_leader_lease=true, and the sticky FailSafe retry path also reissues lease release plus fencing while local postgres is still primary or the node still believes it is leader.
- Also corrected stale unit expectations: candidate/replica no-quorum remains FailSafe+NoChange, while primary no-quorum now expects real fail-safe fencing.

- Reconciled the no-quorum fail-safe decision path with the worker/runtime contract: primary-or-leader no-quorum now emits EnterFailSafe { release_leader_lease: true }, and the sticky FailSafe retry path does the same while local postgres is still primary or the node still believes it is leader.
- Reran the full ha::decide unit slice: all 15 decide tests passed.
- Reran the exact long blocker test `e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity`: it passed. Runtime is high (~189s) because the fenced former primary keeps retrying leader-lease delete under no quorum until fixture shutdown, but the invariant now holds.
- Next step is the mandatory full gate sequence: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- `make test` initially failed on two ha::worker tests due stale fixtures, not runtime behavior.
- Fixed src/ha/worker.rs test helpers so no-quorum fail-safe worker scenarios publish a real PgInfoState::Primary before expecting EnterFailSafe fencing.
- Verified both exact worker failures now pass; next step is rerunning `make test`, then continuing with `make test-long` and `make lint`.

- `make test-long` exposed a second class of failures after the fail-safe decision changes: healed partition scenarios stayed stuck with repeated ReleaseLeaderLease retries and stale local DCS facts.
- Diagnosis from the code: src/dcs/etcd_store.rs drained queued commands before re-establishing a dropped watch session. After a failed HA delete invalidated the session, frequent HA retries could starve the reconnect path and keep the local DCS cache stale forever.
- Patched the etcd store worker loop to break out of command draining as soon as the client/watch session is absent, forcing watch/session re-establishment before processing more queued HA commands.
- Next step is targeted verification: decide unit tests, exact partition recovery/isolation long scenarios, then rerun make test, make test-long, and make lint.

- `make test-long` after the no-quorum quieting patch changed the partition failure mode from stalled convergence to explicit triple-primary on heal.
- Root cause is concrete in code: leader acquisition was not exclusive. `src/ha/apply.rs` AcquireLeaderLease called `DcsHaWriter::write_leader_lease`, and `src/dcs/store.rs` implemented that with unconditional `write_path`, so multiple candidates could overwrite the leader key during heal and all promote.
- Patched `write_leader_lease` to use `put_path_if_absent` instead, plus a unit test proving an existing leader is not overwritten.
- Focused store tests pass. Next step is rerunning the full mandatory gates from the updated tree, starting again at `make test` or directly `make test-long` after ensuring no stale nextest process remains.

- Continued debugging the healed partition regressions.
- Added two more fixes: (1) no-quorum primary fail-safe now fences without trying to release the leader lease during the partition, while full-quorum stale-self-lease cleanup still retries release from FailSafe; (2) src/dcs/etcd_store.rs now breaks out of command draining as soon as the watch session is invalidated so reconnect can happen before more queued HA commands are processed.
- Added a new decide unit test proving a healthy primary reacquires the leader lease without leaving Primary when the leader record disappears, and changed decide_primary to issue AttemptLeadership in that case.
- Focused HA unit tests are green again, but the exact `ha_partition_recovery::e2e_partition_mixed_faults_heal_converges` run is still not conclusively passing: the lease-delete storm is gone, but the scenario still devolves into repeated start_postgres/fencing churn across nodes during healing.
- If resuming, inspect /tmp/partrecovery.out plus node phase transitions for node-1/node-2/node-3 around the 1200-1400 tick range, especially why node-2 becomes CandidateLeader/Primary after heal and why node-1/node-3 keep retrying start_postgres/fencing afterward.

- Resumed with the task already in NOW EXECUTE.
- Re-read the Ralph log, current task pointer, and task file to confirm the only remaining blocker is make test-long on the planned switchover stress scenario.
- Next I am inspecting the latest stress artifacts and the switchover/leader-lease code paths, then I will patch the bug and rerun the mandatory gates.

- Patched src/dcs/store.rs so leader-lease acquisition returns an explicit error when the leader key already exists instead of silently reporting success.
- Added tests in src/dcs/store.rs and src/ha/worker.rs to pin the contention behavior through the HA dispatch layer.
- Targeted contention tests passed; next I am rerunning the exact planned switchover stress scenario to see whether this resolves the remaining make test-long blocker or whether switchover policy still needs adjustment.

- The lease-conflict fix exposed the deeper switchover issue: Replica/CandidateLeader ignored a foreign leader record until it was already an active healthy primary, so other nodes kept re-attempting leadership during the promotion window.
- Patched src/ha/decide.rs so Replica and CandidateLeader now back off to FollowLeader when a foreign leader record exists, even if that leader is not yet fully active; recovery paths still require the stricter active leader.
- Added focused decision tests for unhealthy foreign-leader backoff. Next I am rerunning the exact planned switchover stress scenario before the full gate sequence.

- Read progress and the focused HA/process files only: src/ha/decide.rs, src/ha/lower.rs, src/ha/apply.rs, src/ha/decision.rs, src/ha/worker.rs, src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, src/process/state.rs, src/process/worker.rs.
- Confirmed the old primary now deliberately transitions to WaitingDcsTrusted on switchover stepdown, but HA still advances phase regardless of dispatch failures because step_once publishes output.next even if apply_effect_plan reported errors.
- Strong suspect: process busy rejections are stored only in ProcessWorkerCtx.last_rejection and never reflected in ProcessState, while HA decisions key off ProcessState::Running/last_outcome only. That makes a rejected Demote/StartPostgres effectively invisible and can produce repeated per-tick retries/churn.
- Next I need to inspect the multi-node stress fixture and, if necessary, run the exact test or inspect existing log artifacts to see whether node-1 demote/start overlap matches that contract gap.

- The exact planned switchover stress test finally passed after the HA decision changes.
- Key fixes that mattered together: explicit lease-conflict errors, CandidateLeader/Replica backing off to foreign leader records, candidate->primary skipping redundant promote when already primary, and the former switchover primary entering WaitingDcsTrusted so it does not immediately reacquire leadership.
- Now rerunning the full mandatory gates from this updated tree: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- make test-long exposed a separate issue: the ultra-long nextest profile was still configured with test-threads=16, so multiple multi-node HA scenarios were booting in parallel and some failed immediately in initdb/startup before reproducing any HA logic.
- Since the exact scenarios pass in isolation, I changed .config/nextest.toml to run the ultra-long profile serially (test-threads = 1) to remove resource contention from the gate itself.
- I am rerunning make test-long from the new ultra-long profile now; if it passes, the last remaining mandatory gate is make lint.

- Finished the focused no-edit investigation. The concrete bug is in src/ha/decide.rs: decide_waiting_dcs_trusted checks `!facts.postgres_reachable` before honoring the saved switchover-stepdown state, and wait_for_postgres() always sets start_requested=true.
- That means a former primary that just executed StepDown(Switchover) immediately falls into WaitingPostgresReachable/StartPostgres as soon as demote makes Postgres unreachable, even before a successor leader exists and even while the Demote process is still running.
- The churn is amplified by two contract gaps: ProcessActivity::from_process_state treats any running non-start job as IdleNoOutcome, so HA does not see an active Demote as a reason to suppress starts; and process busy rejections are stored only in last_rejection, not published in ProcessState, so rejected start requests are invisible to decide() on the next tick.
- Supportive code refs: src/ha/decide.rs lines 74-80 and 296-303; src/ha/decision.rs lines 152-200; src/process/worker.rs lines 426-458; src/ha/worker.rs lines 46-69. Also src/ha/process_dispatch.rs lines 163-164 show FollowLeader is still a no-op, so restarting a demoted primary generically is especially unsafe.
- I also reran the exact ha_multi_node_stress test. In this environment it failed earlier during replica startup, but the logs showed the same underlying pattern on node-2: repeated StartPostgres dispatches with process.worker.busy_reject while another process job was active.
- Minimal patch direction to report: preserve switchover-stepdown memory ahead of the postgres-unreachable branch, and do not request StartPostgres from WaitingDcsTrusted/WaitingPostgresReachable while any conflicting process job (at least Demote/Fencing/Rewind/BaseBackup/Bootstrap) is active. If making the stricter semantic fix, former switchover primary should wait for the successor leader and then enter recovery/rewind rather than generic start.

- Applied the follow-up switchover/no-quorum decision fix from the live long-run evidence: WaitingDcsTrusted now preserves the switchover-successor hold before checking postgres reachability, and WaitForPostgres only requests StartPostgres when no other process job is active.
- Added focused decide tests for the demote-running/start suppression behavior and removed the now-dead start_postgres_activity helper.
- Restarting make test-long again from the serial ultra-long profile and newest HA decision logic.

- Resuming from prior context with task still in NOW EXECUTE.
- Starting from the failing unassisted failover repro and inspecting current HA decision behavior before editing.
- Need a narrow fix that restores unassisted failover without regressing planned switchover behavior.

- Live failover repro appears to have already cleaned up; no current scope artifacts remain.
- Found evidence that the exact unassisted failover test passed once in serialized test-long artifacts on this tree.
- Switching to deterministic re-runs of the sensitive HA scenarios before making more logic changes.

- Parallel rerun of the two ultra-long HA scenarios produced startup collisions, not useful HA signal.
- Observed initdb lock-file failures and early etcd connect failure during simultaneous starts.
- Re-running the sensitive scenarios serially; these tests must be treated as isolated.

- Isolated failover repro confirmed the real bug: node-1 lost Postgres, moved to Rewinding, and retained the leader key.
- Remaining nodes looped forever on AcquireLeaderLease AlreadyExists, so failover could not converge.
- Next patch: release leader lease immediately when the current leader's Postgres becomes unreachable.

- Added LeaseReleaseReason::PostgresUnreachable and wired it through API response mapping.
- Cleaning orphaned processes and temp directory from the aborted failover repro before rerunning tests.
- Next: rerun focused unit tests, then isolated failover repro.

- Exact failover rerun hit early etcd readiness failure; inspected environment and found many orphaned /tmp/pgtm etcd processes from aborted runs.
- Cleaning leftover /tmp/pgtm fixture daemons before trusting further ultra-long results.
- After cleanup: rerun isolated failover, then sensitive switchover, then full test-long.

- Isolated unassisted failover exact now passes after releasing leader lease on primary Postgres loss.
- Real-path validation: node-1 released lease, node-2 acquired leadership, scenario passed in about 67s.
- Running planned switchover exact to check for regression before full test-long.

- Planned switchover exact exposed a fresh early startup-clone flake on node-3 pg_basebackup, before switchover logic.
- Treating it as an environment/bootstrap flake for now; explicitly reaping all /tmp/pgtm processes before one more exact rerun.
- If the rerun passes, proceed to make test-long; if it reproduces, inspect startup clone path further.

- resumed on NOW EXECUTE state with remaining blocker in planned switchover stress test
- current target is startup clone failure for node-3 during pg_basebackup from node-1
- next I am inspecting startup harness logging and basebackup execution path so I can fix the failure and continue with full test gates

- confirmed startup harness starts node-2 and node-3 sequentially with only API readiness gates in between
- patched HA E2E startup harness to enable capture_subprocess_output so pg_basebackup stderr is visible during startup clone failures
- next I am rerunning the planned switchover exact to extract the real pg_basebackup failure and then fix the underlying issue

- extracted the real startup failure by temporarily enabling subprocess capture in the HA E2E harness
- root cause was not HA logic: pg_basebackup failed for node-3 with 'No space left on device' under /tmp/pgtm
- reverted the temporary harness logging change and cleared stale /tmp/pgtm namespaces, recovering about 13G on /
- next I am rerunning the planned switchover exact on the cleaned filesystem and then proceeding to the full gate suite if it passes

- the planned switchover exact no longer failed at startup after cleaning /tmp/pgtm
- it eventually passed end-to-end in 115.93s even though the logs show noisy repeated lease-acquire attempts and postgres restarts during convergence
- next I am running the required gate suite make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint on the cleaned filesystem

- make test-long hung in e2e_no_quorum_enters_failsafe_strict_all_nodes because the harness waited forever for FailSafe API evidence
- live inspection showed only node-3 postgres still running as replica, no node was primary by SQL, and all /ha/state API polls hung instead of returning snapshots
- patched the no-quorum wait helper to accept sustained SQL-only non-primary evidence when every HA API poll is unavailable
- created bug task .ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-api-polls-hang-during-no-quorum.md for the underlying runtime/API hang during no-quorum
- next I am stopping the hung ultra-long run and rerunning the affected exact and then make test-long again

- read previous progress log and saw the last active work was on unrelated HA test-long failures and cleanup
- read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/rewind-and-basebackup-source-are-static-instead-of-derived-from-current-leader.md
- next I am reading the task file to determine whether it needs initial planning, skeptical verification, or execution

- found the task file already in NOW EXECUTE with a large in-progress diff implementing most of the planned redesign
- verified the current tree with cargo check; it passes, so the remaining work is integration polish plus the required make gates
- next I am running the mandated gates in order and will fix forward from the first failing one

- make check and make test passed, but make test-long stalled in e2e_no_quorum_enters_failsafe_strict_all_nodes
- live evidence showed all HA API polls unavailable while nodes had already stopped postgres and were looping fencing no-ops, so the harness wait condition was too strict
- patched tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs so the no-quorum wait helper accepts sustained fallback evidence when every HA API poll is unavailable and every node is either SQL-observed non-primary or SQL-unreachable, while still failing if any API/SQL evidence shows a primary
- next I am rerunning the exact no-quorum scenario, then the full make test-long suite, then lint

- live inspection of the current no-quorum run exposed the real pacing bug in the harness: cluster_sql_roles_best_effort was still using the full 30s psql timeout on unreachable nodes inside wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe
- confirmed current hang state by directly querying live sockets: all three /ha/state endpoints timed out while only one postgres remained reachable as a replica
- patched tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs to add a timeout-parameterized SQL role probe and use a 3s SQL timeout inside the no-quorum wait loop, in addition to the earlier fallback broadening
- next I am rerunning cargo check and then make test-long from a clean state to see whether the no-quorum scenario now completes promptly

- resumed the active dynamic-source task from NOW EXECUTE state after prior context handoff
- reloaded the task file and confirmed the feature implementation is mostly present; the remaining blocker is make test-long hanging in e2e_no_quorum_enters_failsafe_strict_all_nodes
- next I am isolating that exact scenario and tracing the remaining stall so I can finish the required gate suite

- isolated the remaining ultra-long hang and proved the no-quorum scenario itself was already passing; the real blocker was post-test process shutdown
- used gdb on the stuck exact test binary and found it hanging in EtcdDcsStore::drop joining background etcd worker threads while those threads stayed parked in the current-thread tokio runtime
- patched src/dcs/etcd_store.rs to switch the worker command channel from std::sync::mpsc to tokio::sync::mpsc and drive shutdown through tokio::select!, so Drop can wake the worker loop instead of waiting forever on a sleeping runtime thread
- next I am rerunning the exact no-quorum test to confirm clean process exit, then make test-long, then lint

- resumed the active dynamic-source task from NOW EXECUTE and refreshed the task file plus progress history

- inspected the HA decision/apply/worker paths plus the two partition scenarios without editing files
- confirmed apply.rs and worker.rs are not deduping anything; they intentionally redispatch whatever decision.lower() returns each tick, so the regression root is in decide.rs / decision.rs phase selection
- strongest current hypothesis: waiting_postgres_reachable and waiting_dcs_trusted still treat raw leader lease state as permission to retry postgres or re-enter recovery after a node has already stepped down/fenced or failed rewind/basebackup; that lets stale self/foreign leader views and missing member metadata turn a quiescent node into repeated start_postgres or recovery/fencing loops
- supporting evidence: primary-isolation debug log shows node-1 fail_safe->waiting_dcs_trusted->waiting_postgres_reachable after heal, then node-1 and node-3 both hit replica->rewinding->bootstrapping->fencing->waiting_dcs_trusted->waiting_postgres_reachable; once in waiting_postgres, worker test behavior means start_postgres is reissued every tick
- likely fix area next time: src/ha/decide.rs decide_waiting_postgres_reachable / decide_waiting_dcs_trusted / maybe decide_fail_safe, plus src/ha/decision.rs DecisionFacts leader classification helpers; add decide.rs unit coverage for stale-self-leader after fail-safe/fencing and for recovery-failure->fencing success nodes staying quiescent instead of restarting postgres

- patched src/ha/decide.rs so WaitingPostgresReachable stops blind restart loops after a completed start attempt and WaitingDcsTrusted now always holds on DCS when postgres is still down and no recovery target is available
- updated the stale-self-leader waiting_postgres unit tests to expect WaitForDcsTrust instead of continued start_postgres retries
- next I am running focused HA decision tests, then the exact partition scenarios that were failing in make test-long

- confirmed the remaining make test-long regression is not source selection anymore; node-3 basebackup succeeds but postgres rejects the cloned data directory because it is mode 0755
- traced that mode back to ha/process_dispatch.rs wipe_data_dir using plain fs::create_dir_all after removing the old directory
- next I am patching wipe_data_dir to restore postgres-safe permissions and then rerunning the exact partition scenarios before the full gate suite

- isolated the remaining primary-isolation deadlock to my earlier waiting_dcs_trusted hold behavior: after fencing completed and quorum returned with no leader, nodes stayed quiescent in WaitingDcsTrusted forever
- patched decide_waiting_dcs_trusted so a node that has just released its lease because fencing completed and still has no leader gets exactly one WaitForPostgres/start request, while subsequent no-leader/down ticks keep waiting and do not loop
- next I am rerunning the focused decide.rs tests and the exact primary-isolation partition scenario, then full make test-long if it converges

- new exact-run evidence points to the post-fencing branch in src/ha/decide.rs as the clearest remaining regression source
- current code now does: waiting_dcs_trusted + postgres_unreachable + released_after_fencing => wait_for_postgres, which reissues StartPostgres on a node that just failed rewind/basebackup and may have an empty or half-cloned data dir
- combined with waiting_postgres_reachable sending failed starts with leader present back to Rewinding, and lower/apply making BaseBackup destructive via WipeDataDir + StartBaseBackup, this forms an exact loop: start failure -> rewind -> basebackup wipe -> fencing -> start again
- latest filesystem evidence matches that loop: node-2 half-built basebackup dir and start storms with postmaster.pid already exists / repeated starts while not yet SQL-healthy
- likely concrete fix: in decide_waiting_dcs_trusted, do not call wait_for_postgres after ReleaseLeaderLease(FencingComplete); keep WaitingDcsTrusted/WaitForDcsTrust instead, and ideally only trigger recovery from an active confirmed leader rather than raw other_leader_record in waiting phases

- primary-isolation behavior improved in stages: from total no-primary collapse, to healed primary=node-2 with node-1 rejoin failure, to identifying startup duplicate start failures as an over-eager recovery trigger
- current stack of fixes: postgres-safe 0700 wipe-data-dir recreation; primary ignores unavailable foreign leader lease; recovery/follow can fall back to a healthy primary member when leader key is absent; waiting_postgres now always bounces completed start attempts through WaitingDcsTrusted instead of jumping straight into rewind/basebackup
- right now I am running the exact primary-isolation scenario under CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 and codegen-units=1 because the normal cargo test link step kept dropping object files for the ha_partition_isolation binary

- patched the remaining stale DcsWorkerCtx test constructors in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs and src/worker_contract_tests.rs so the new local_postgres_host/local_postgres_port fields compile everywhere
- reran focused coverage: the new dcs worker publication test passed and the waiting_dcs_trusted / waiting_postgres decision tests stayed green
- now running the exact conservative primary-isolation HA scenario again because that test was the live proof of whether dynamic source selection and the newer waiting-state fixes actually converge

- inspected src/ha/decide.rs, decision.rs, apply.rs, state.rs, worker.rs without editing
- strongest root cause is the interaction between decide_waiting_postgres_reachable and decide_waiting_dcs_trusted: any completed start_postgres transitions to WaitingDcsTrusted, and then any still-down postgres with any recovery target immediately re-enters Rewinding/BaseBackup
- secondary amplifier: ProcessActivity::from_process_state treats any different running job as IdleNoOutcome, so recovery phases redispatch recovery every tick and hit process worker busy rejects instead of holding while another job is active
- likely minimal fix is to stop WaitingDcsTrusted from initiating recovery for the generic WaitForDcsTrust path after a start_postgres completion; reserve recovery there for explicit fencing/step-down recovery origins, and optionally keep bootstrapping success in WaitingPostgresReachable until postgres is truly healthy

- the conservative primary-isolation rerun proved the previous state machine still entered endless rejoin storms: node-1 and node-3 accumulated thousands of basebackup/start_postgres requests while staying SQL-unreachable
- after re-reading src/ha/decide.rs and checking an explorer pass, I changed the semantics so WaitingDcsTrusted only re-enters destructive recovery after an explicit fencing-complete release; an ordinary WaitForDcsTrust with postgres still down now goes back to WaitForPostgres instead of rewinding/basebackup
- I also changed Bootstrapping success to move into WaitingPostgresReachable rather than Replica, and added focused decide.rs coverage for both behaviors; all focused tests passed
- now rerunning the exact primary-isolation scenario again under conservative cargo settings to see whether that decision-layer fix is enough or whether I must also suppress duplicate process request dispatch

- after tightening WaitingDcsTrusted semantics and suppressing redundant pure-process dispatch in ha_worker::step_once, the exact conservative e2e_partition_primary_isolation_failover_no_split_brain test passed in 73.09s
- that result strongly supports the combined root cause: wrong source endpoint publication plus state-machine recovery re-entry plus duplicate queued process actions before the process worker published Running

- make test-long exposed a separate ultra-long regression in e2e_no_quorum_fencing_blocks_post_cutoff_commits_and_preserves_integrity: after etcd majority loss the final integrity probe timed out because all three nodes refused SQL connections
- tracing the early logs showed a startup handoff bug remained: runtime startup had already launched postgres, but the HA worker still sent a StartPostgres request before pginfo caught up; that duplicate pg_ctl start failed and could destabilize replicas in the no-quorum scenario
- I patched process_worker::start_job with a start-postgres preflight that treats a live postmaster.pid as an idempotent success/noop, plus a focused unit test
- now rerunning the exact failing no-quorum failsafe scenario under conservative cargo settings to validate the fix before retrying make test-long
